body.title: [in_progress] Config layer: local bootstrap profile + repo secret-free post-processor
body.intention: A new install boots its identity/credentials from one local file that sources a repo-tracked, secret-free post-processor; nothing identity-related is baked into code.
body.description:
**Problem:** Identity (DB url, instance name, host) is hardcoded across the repo. Introduce the config layer that replaces it.

**Shape:** (1) LOCAL `~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh` (gitignored-by-location, the ONLY plaintext secret on disk) sets the bootstrap vars: UU_DB_USER, UU_DB_PASSWORD, UU_DB_IP, UU_HOME_DB_IP (optional), IGOR_NAME — initial IGOR_NAME value stays the CURRENT 'Igor-wild-0001' (behavior-preserving; rename is a later ticket). It ends by sourcing the repo processor. (2) REPO `bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh` (NO secrets): composes UU_HOME_DB_URL=postgresql://${UU_DB_USER}:${UU_DB_PASSWORD}@${UU_HOME_DB_IP:-${UU_DB_IP}}/${IGOR_NAME}; sets IGOR_SWARM_NAME=$(hostname) and CC session names; then pulls any further secrets from the vault and exports them. (3) .bashrc reduced to a single `source ~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh` line.

**Affected files:** new bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh; new ~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh (local, not committed); ~/.bashrc (one line); .gitignore check; a committed `bin/uu_bash_profile.sh.example` template documenting the bootstrap vars.
**Design rules:** no-creds-in-source (processor MUST contain no secrets); fail-soft (DB/vault down must NOT break the shell); test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the two scripts, the var scheme, the .example template, fail-soft vault pull WITH caching (no DB round-trip on every shell init). OUT — de-hardcoding the code readers (separate sweeps); rotation.
**Test plan:** sourcing the processor with the bootstrap vars set exports a correct UU_HOME_DB_URL + IGOR_SWARM_NAME==$(hostname); with the DB down it still returns 0 and exports the bootstrap/derived vars (no hang, no error); the processor file contains no credential literal (grep).
**Completion criteria:** `bash -c 'source bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh; echo $UU_HOME_DB_URL'` composes the URL from the bootstrap vars; `grep -E 'choose_a_password|:[^@/]+@' bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh` is empty; with DB unreachable the source returns within ~1s and rc 0.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
